raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 689 will allow for survivors of domestic violence to seek help via text and other computer-based technologies.",
    "The Covid-19 Pandemic has caused a dramatic increase in Californian’s rates of domestic violence.",
    "Domestic violence hotlines received approximately 13 calls a minute before the pandemic.",
    "The National Domestic Violence Hotline saw a 9% increase in calls, texts, and chats during the pandemic.",
    "Under the current California Code, domestic violence centers are limited to a definition of phone-based hotlines that excludes chat and text.",
    "Modernizing domestic violence shelter requirements to include other technological platforms will help more victims.",
    "The state can collect more accurate data about domestic abuse by expanding technological platforms.",
    "Texting or live chat options for crisis lines provide additional access points for survivors of domestic violence.",
    "A secure chat line platform has proven effective for communicating with youth and young adults experiencing domestic violence.",
    "Chat and text-based hotlines allow survivors to connect with advocates when they cannot safely call a hotline.",
    "Expanding access points to include chat and text-based options is key to breaking down barriers to survivor safety."
  ]
}
```
